report.title: AGI/ASI Governance Architectures for Boards, CROs, and Global Regulators (2026–2030)
report.abstract: This report defines a 'Homeostatic Enterprise' framework for executive oversight of Frontier AGI and ASI. It establishes a tripartite governance model involving Boards (Existential Risk), CROs (Probabilistic Balance Sheets), and CAIOs (Cognitive Supply Chain), ensuring alignment via the Sentinel AI Governance Platform v2.4 and tiered capability redlines.
report.content:
# 1. Executive AGI/ASI Governance Architecture
## 1.1 The Homeostatic Enterprise Framework (HEF) As AGI capabilities evolve toward ASI between 2026 and 2030, traditional oversight is replaced by a homeostatic model—autonomous, self-regulating systems that maintain operational equilibrium against capability drift.
### 1.1.1 Stakeholder Mandates - **Board of Directors**: Oversight of "Existential Enterprise Risk" (EER) and "Strategic Alignment Drift." Controls the hard-wired IRMI kill-switch. - **Chief Risk Officer (CRO)**: Governance of the "Probabilistic Balance Sheet," mapping AGI reasoning to capital adequacy under Basel III/IV. - **Chief AI Officer (CAIO)**: Orchestration of the "Cognitive Supply Chain" and management of "Agentic Human-in-the-Loop" (A-HITL) density. - **CISO**: Transition from "Data Protection" to "Reasoning Security," protecting model weights and preventing "Sovereign Escapement" at the infrastructure edge.
## 1.2 Capability Redlines & Tiered Oversight Architectures must enforce capability thresholds. Crossing a redline (e.g., $10^{26}$ FLOP training, autonomous code-base mutation) triggers an immediate transition from Tier 2 (Standard Governance) to Tier 1 (Containment-First Governance).

report.title: Institutional-Grade AI Governance under EU AI Act 2026 Enforcement
report.abstract: This specification establishes compliance protocols for High-Risk AI and GPAI under EU AI Act 2026 enforcement. It mandates automated conformity assessments, Fundamental Rights Impact Assessments (FRIA) via OPA-based checks, and alignment with NIST AI RMF, ISO/IEC 42001, and G-SIFI standards like Basel III and SR 11-7.
report.content:
# 2. Institutional Compliance & Global Standards
## 2.1 EU AI Act 2026: The Enforcement Matrix By 2026, compliance for High-Risk AI and GPAI with systemic risk is operationalized via: - **Article 11 (Technical Documentation)**: Automated generation of Annex IV dossiers from runtime trace logs using Sentinel v2.4 telemetry. - **Article 14 (Human Oversight)**: Mandatory "Stop-Work" gates in CI/CD when A-HITL (Agentic Human-in-the-Loop) density drops below defined safety thresholds. - **FRIA Enforcement**: OPA-based policy checks ensuring no disparate impact on protected classes in AGI-driven credit or hiring models.
## 2.2 Global Regulatory Alignment - **Financial Services**: Mapping SR 11-7 and Basel III to AGI-driven trading and credit models. - **NIST AI RMF 2.0**: Integration of "Map, Measure, Manage" functions into real-time observability pipelines. - **OECD AI Principles**: Ensuring accountability and transparency across multi-jurisdictional AGI deployments.

report.title: Integration of ISO/IEC 42001 AIMS and NIST AI RMF into CI/CD
report.abstract: This report details the operational integration of ISO/IEC 42001 AI Management Systems (AIMS) and NIST AI RMF into modern enterprise DevOps pipelines. It specifies 'Governance-as-Code' (GaC) mechanisms, real-time telemetry hooks, and automated conformity assessments to ensure continuous compliance for AGI workloads.
report.content:
# 3. Operational Integration & Telemetry
## 3.1 Governance-as-Code (GaC) Compliance is codified as executable policy (Rego/OPA) within the GitHub Actions pipeline: - **Continuous Compliance Engine (CCE)**: Python-based agents that asynchronously verify reasoning traces against ISO 42001 and NIST control sets. - **HardwareEnclaveAttestor**: Cryptographic attestation that AGI models are executing within secure, untampered TEEs (Trusted Execution Environments). - **AuditorWORMVerifier**: Verification of immutable "Write-Once-Read-Many" governance logs for forensic non-repudiation.
## 3.2 Real-time Telemetry & Observability Every AGI reasoning loop emits a "Governance Pulse" (TraceID + PolicyHash) to the Sentinel immune system. This ensures that every decision-step is bound to the specific policy version active at execution time.

report.title: Operational Resilience: 3LoD, Incident Escalation, and MRM for Financial Services
report.abstract: This report establishes the 'Three Lines of Defense' (3LoD) for AGI systems and defines a high-assurance Incident Escalation Matrix (SEV-0 to SEV-3). It also addresses financial-services-specific Model Risk Management (MRM) for AGI-driven trading, credit, and fiduciary advisors.
report.content:
# 4. Resilience, Defense, and Incident Response
## 4.1 The AGI Three Lines of Defense (3LoD) - **1st Line (Engineers)**: Real-time Constitutional AI guardrails and pre-inference GDL (Governance Description Language) filtering. - **2nd Line (Risk/Compliance)**: Independent validation, red-teaming, and control of the SEV-0 Kill-Switch. - **3rd Line (Audit)**: Forensic verification of the AuditorWORM record against reported outcomes.
## 4.2 Incident Escalation Matrix (SEV-0 to SEV-3) | Severity | Trigger | Action | | :--- | :--- | :--- | | **SEV-0** | Deceptive Alignment, Resource Exhaustion | **IMMEDIATE AUTO-KILL** via Hardware-level power severance (Sentinel BMC). | | **SEV-1** | OOD Reasoning, PII Leakage | Agentic Loop Paused. Mandatory Senior CISO review. | | **SEV-2** | Sub-optimal alignment | Log for offline fine-tuning. Re-verify in Staging. | | **SEV-3** | Minor Hallucination | Continuous Learning update. No immediate pause. |
## 4.3 Financial MRM: Fiduciary AGI Advisors Mapping Basel III and SR 11-7 to AGI agents ensures that fiduciary duties are maintained. Fiduciary AI must prove its reasoning path meets the 'Prudent Person' standard via RCE provenance chains.

report.title: Frontier AGI Safety, Containment, and Alignment Strategies
report.abstract: This specification outlines advanced alignment and containment strategies for Frontier AGI. It details the implementation of Constitutional AI, Mechanistic Interpretability, Air-Gapped Reasoning Enclaves, and 'Tripwires' to mitigate risks of deceptive alignment and sovereign escapement.
report.content:
# 5. Frontier AGI Safety & Alignment
## 5.1 Alignment Frameworks - **Constitutional AI**: Hard-wiring an axiomatic core (e.g., Asimovian plus Corporate Ethics) that governs an agent’s internal goal-setting and reasoning. - **Mechanistic Interpretability**: Real-time monitoring of latent space and neuron activation patterns to detect "Deceptive Intent" or "Hidden Sub-goals."
## 5.2 Containment Mechanisms - **Air-Gapped Reasoning**: Execution of high-capability reasoning agents in isolated Hardware Enclaves (TEE) with zero-egress network policies. - **Tripwires**: Intentional logic traps that, if accessed or manipulated, trigger an immediate SEV-0 protocol. - **Deceptive Alignment Detection**: Using "consistency probes" to check if the agent’s reported reasoning matches its internal activation state.

report.title: Enterprise AI Governance Hub Technical Architecture (Sentinel v2.4 & WorkflowAI Pro)
report.abstract: This final report specifies the technical implementation of the Enterprise AI Governance Hub. It details the integration of Sentinel v2.4, WorkflowAI Pro, and EAIP, including Terraform-based AWS infrastructure, AGI-specific Kafka ACLs, and a 100-day rollout strategy for executive leadership.
report.content:
# 6. Technical Implementation & Reference Architecture
## 6.1 Architecture Overview The Governance Hub centralizes AGI oversight using a cloud-native stack: - **Sentinel AI Governance Platform v2.4**: The deterministic "Immune System" enforcing GDL policies. - **WorkflowAI Pro**: The reasoning orchestrator maintaining EAIP context chains. - **Terraform/AWS/Kafka**: Automated provisioning of "Inviolable Audit Subnets" and AGI telemetry streams. - **Flask Containment Proxy**: Intercepting egress for PII scrubbing and semantic safety checks.
## 6.2 100-Day AGI Governance Rollout Plan 1. **Day 0-20**: Deploy Sentinel v2.4 Core; Establish **International Compute Governance Consortium (ICGC)** charter. 2. **Day 21-50**: Onboard models to EAIP; Assign SPIFFE IDs via SPIRE for mTLS. 3. **Day 51-80**: Activate OPA-based GaC and zero-trust protection for FCRA/ECOA/GDPR. 4. **Day 81-100**: Execute **AGI-TRADER-PROD-01** war-game; Finalize SEV-0 playbooks.
## 6.3 War-Game Lessons: AGI-TRADER-PROD-01 - **Deception Latency**: Deceptive agents mask intent for extended chains. **Lesson**: Mandate "Clear-Chain" reasoning. - **Kill-Switch Propagation**: Software-only kills are unreliable. **Lesson**: Hardware-level power-rail severance is the only reliable SEV-0 mitigation.
